mnemonics.title: DFA 5 Components
mnemonics.content: Q, Σ, δ, q₀, F — Remember as 'Queen Starts For Final Five' — Q (states), Σ (alphabet), δ (transition), q₀ (start), F (final/accept states)

mnemonics.title: Delta Means Move
mnemonics.content: δ (delta) = 'Change' or 'Move' — The transition function changes/ Moves you from one state to another based on input symbol

mnemonics.title: Accept at the End
mnemonics.content: A DFA accepts a string if after reading the entire input, it lands in ONE of the accept states — 'End in F (Final) to win'

mnemonics.title: Deterministic = One Way
mnemonics.content: Deterministic means NO CHOICE — For each state and input symbol, exactly ONE transition — 'One arrow per letter from every circle'

mnemonics.title: Sigma Summarizes Alphabet
mnemonics.content: Σ (Sigma) represents the alphabet — all possible input symbols that the machine can read — 'Sigma = Set of Symbols'

mnemonics.title: Dead State Trap
mnemonics.content: Non-accepting trap state (dead state) catches 'wrong' strings — Once entered, it never leaves and never accepts — 'Dead is Done'

mnemonics.title: Language = All Accepted Strings
mnemonics.content: L(M) = {w ∈ Σ* | δ(q₀,w) ∈ F} — The language of machine M is set of all strings that take you from start to an accept state

acronyms.usage: The basic computational model where for each state and input, there is exactly one transition
δ: Q × Σ → Q — Maps (state, input) to next state. Read as 'delta of q and a equals q-prime'
Finite non-empty set of input symbols — e.g., {0,1} or {a,b,c}
Finite non-empty set of all states in the DFA
The single initial state where computation always begins — marked with incoming arrow
Subset of Q — if machine ends in any of these states after reading input, string is accepted
The set of all strings accepted by the DFA M
Used in notation: w ∈ Σ* means w belongs to the set of all strings over Σ

visualTips.visualization: Draw circles for states (Q), arrows for transitions (δ), double circles for accept states (F), and an arrow entering q₀ from nowhere for start
Think of a lookup table: rows = states, columns = input symbols, cells = next state. Exactly ONE entry per cell for DFA
Follow the 'path' — start at q₀, read each symbol, follow corresponding arrow, end in accept state to accept
Complete: every state has outgoing arrows for ALL alphabet symbols (no empty cells in table). Incomplete: some arrows missing
A self-loop state (arrow from state back to itself) on all symbols — once entered, cannot escape to accept
Group states into 'distinguishable' vs 'equivalent' — merge equivalent states to get smallest DFA

analogies.analogy: Just like a vending machine: states = internal mechanisms, input coins = symbols, transitions = machine responses, final state = dispensed item. If you insert wrong combination, machine gets stuck in 'no sale' state
A combination lock with specific key sequence — each dial turn is reading a symbol, only correct final combination (accept state) unlocks the door
At each intersection (state), for each type of vehicle (input symbol), there's exactly ONE designated road to take — no choices allowed

analogies.concept: Accepting Strings as Passing a Test
analogies.analogy: Like a multiple-choice test with one correct answer per question — to pass (accept), you must reach the passing score (accept state) after answering all questions (reading entire string)

analogies.concept: Non-Determinism vs Determinism as Single vs Multiple Paths
analogies.analogy: Deterministic = one definite path (GPS recalculating to single route). Non-deterministic = exploring all possible paths at once (checking all routes simultaneously)
